pages.raw_text:
11,624 who are working on the
barns will be called off the job to-
day. 1t is said that the delegates
to the Central Labor union will
stand by the action of the executive
committee of the body and will ask
the union to withdraw it's dele
gates If the men remain at work.
1t is also likely that charges will
be preferred against the union, and
the American Federation asked to
revoke its charter should the men
continue at work.


1t is not denied that Contractors
Johnson and Hughes employ union
men. There is no objection to them
by the uion people; the fight is
against the Washington Water
Power company, as part of the
work was done by that concern
with non-union men, and what 1t
could not do was contracted for
The uions demand that all the work
be union or non-union.
